section1.title: 1. Task Publishing
section1.item1: After adding a dataset to a project, if the dataset's annotator_id is empty, it will automatically appear in the "Available Tasks" list
section1.item2: Annotators can view and claim tasks on the "Available Tasks" page. After claiming, the dataset will be assigned to that user
section1.item3: You can set a dataset's category to match users' species tags for targeted task assignment
section1.item4: Set evaluation purpose and deadline in project metadata, which will be displayed in task details
section1.item5: Annotation configs are automatically displayed as evaluation dimensions in task info, helping annotators understand requirements
section1.item6: After tasks are published, users can see complete information: dataset name, task description, target annotation count, project info, evaluation dimensions, etc.

section2.title: 2. Project Creation and Management
section2.item1: Create new projects with name, description, version, status, category, tags, etc.
section2.item2: Edit project info including data source, evaluation purpose, deadline, and other metadata
section2.item3: Set extra fields to display in the project, which will be inherited by datasets under the project
section2.item4: Delete projects (after deletion, datasets' project_id will be set to NULL)

section3.title: 3. Project Import
section3.item1: Batch import projects from JSONL files, support uploading multiple files at once
section3.item2: Two import modes: create new project or import into existing project
section3.item3: Customize dataset name for each file, or use unified prefix naming convention
section3.item4: Set project metadata during import (version, category, tags, data source, etc.)

section4.title: 4. Dataset Management
section4.item1: Add and import datasets in the project
section4.item2: Edit dataset info (name, description, annotator, etc.)
section4.item3: Remove datasets from projects (doesn't delete datasets, just removes the association)
section4.item4: Export annotation results for all datasets in the project

section5.title: 5. Annotation Config Management
section5.item1: Add annotation configs to the project, which will be inherited by datasets under the project
section5.item2: If a dataset already has its own config, it won't inherit project's config (dataset config has priority)
section5.item3: Adjust the display order of annotation configs in the project (move up/down)
section5.item4: Remove annotation configs from the project
section5.item5: Support various annotation types: binary, text, score, category, single/multiple choice, etc.

section6.title: 6. Annotation Work
section6.item1: Select and annotate datasets under the project in the annotation workbench
section6.item2: Use annotation configs associated with the project or dataset during annotation
section6.item3: View project statistics (dataset count, config count, etc.)

section7.title: 7. Data Export
section7.item1: Export annotation results for all datasets in the project
section7.item2: Support JSON and CSV export formats
section7.item3: Exported ZIP file contains all datasets, each file named using the dataset name
section7.item4: JSON format includes complete annotation results and QA pair information
section7.item5: CSV format provides flattened annotation results for easy data analysis

bestPractices.title: Usage Recommendations
bestPractices.configOrderDesc: It's recommended to configure annotation configs for the project before importing datasets, so datasets can automatically inherit the project's configs
bestPractices.inheritanceDesc: Datasets automatically inherit project configs, no need to configure each dataset individually; if a dataset needs special configs, add them separately to that dataset
bestPractices.batchImportDesc: Use project import to quickly create projects and import multiple datasets, suitable for batch processing scenarios
bestPractices.backupDesc: Regularly export annotation results for backup and analysis, recommended to export at important annotation milestones
bestPractices.managementDesc: Properly use project categories, tags, and status for easier project management and lookup

config.minMaxError: Max score must be greater than min score

config.deleteConfigConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this annotation config? This operation cannot be undone.

usage.title: Annotation Config Usage Guide
usage.section1Item1: Create and edit annotation configs with name, description, type, etc.
usage.section1Item2: Set configs as required or optional; required configs must be completed during annotation
usage.section1Item3: View the number of related datasets to understand config usage
usage.section1Item4: Delete annotation configs (if config has results, clear them first)
usage.section2Item1: Score: Set min score, max score and score step for numerical scoring
usage.section2Item2: Category: Define multiple category options, one per line
usage.section2Item3: Text: Support text input with optional max length limit
usage.section2Item4: Single Choice: Define multiple options, user can only select one
usage.section2Item5: Multi Choice: Define multiple options, user can select multiple
usage.section2Item6: Binary: Yes/No type annotation, customizable True/False labels
usage.section3Item1: Annotation configs can be linked to datasets to provide annotation rules
usage.section3Item2: Annotation configs can be linked to projects, datasets under projects automatically inherit project configs
usage.section3Item3: If a dataset has its own config, it won't inherit project config (dataset config takes priority)
usage.section3Item4: One config can be linked to multiple datasets or projects
usage.section4Item1: Datasets prioritize their own annotation configs
usage.section4Item2: If a dataset has no config, it automatically inherits its project's annotation config
usage.section4Item3: Inherited configs display and function normally in annotation workbench
usage.section4Item4: Inheritance is dynamic; modifying project config affects all datasets inheriting it
usage.section5Item1: View the number of annotation results linked to a config
usage.section5Item2: Clear all annotation results for a config (irreversible)
usage.section5Item3: If a config has annotation results, editing or deletion is not allowed
usage.section5Item4: After clearing annotation results, the config can be edited or deleted again
usage.section6Item1: Annotation workbench displays appropriate interface based on dataset or project config
usage.section6Item2: Required configs must be completed before submission
usage.section6Item3: Different config types display different controls (sliders, dropdowns, text boxes, etc.)
usage.section6Item4: Config descriptions display in annotation interface to help annotators understand requirements
usage.suggestion1: Config Naming: Use clear, descriptive config names for easy management and lookup
usage.suggestion2: Config Description: Add detailed descriptions to help annotators understand annotation requirements
usage.suggestion3: Required Setting: Set configs as required or optional based on actual needs
usage.suggestion4: Project-level Config: For configs shared by multiple datasets in a project, add at project level for unified management
usage.suggestion5: Dataset-level Config: For specific dataset needs, add config at dataset level
usage.suggestion6: Config Reuse: Reuse the same config for identical annotation needs to avoid duplication
usage.suggestion7: Config Modification: Before modifying, check if config has annotation results; clear results first if it does
